content: To prevent iodine deficiency, ensure a daily reliable source of iodine in the diet.
Seafood, such as fish, shellfish, and seaweed, are reliable sources of iodine.
Celtic sea salt and pink Himalayan salt are not good sources of iodine.
Cruciferous vegetables, including broccoli, kale, and cauliflower, contain compounds that inhibit iodine uptake.
Moderation is key when consuming cruciferous vegetables and other foods that release small amounts of cyanide.
Symptoms of hypothyroidism include fatigue, brain fog, puffy eyes, cold hands and feet, and hair loss.
